text: Machine learning is a type of artificial intelligence where computers learn from data. In supervised learning, the computer uses training data, which includes both input information and correct outputs. By analyzing this data, the computer builds a model. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions about new, unseen data. For example, a model trained on pictures of cats and dogs can predict whether a new picture contains a cat or a dog. The model's performance is measured by its accuracy—how often its predictions are correct. The higher the accuracy, the better the model has learned.
